title: Mary Nomura Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454977 .

Oral history with Mary Nomura, who was a teenager living in Los Angeles at the time of Pearl Harbor. As orphaned children, Mary and her siblings were sent to the concentration camp in Manzanar, California, where she gained a reputation as the "Songbird of Manzanar." Mary met her future husband, Shi Nomura, in camp at a dance. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
